race.comment: ARK SEA (6) is a smart filly who tuned up for this with a victory over older rivals at Wagga, leading to score by 4m. Has enough speed to go forward from this barrier and the only time she raced at this circuit she was runner-up against the boys in last year's Protostar. Big chance. SPARKLING SEA (9) was dominant in working forward to find the front before cruising home by nearly 26m at this circuit and distance last week. Draws on the back row but with a touch of luck and a repeat of that performance she'll be tough to stop. RIPPLES NZ (10) is another that will need things to go her way from the second line but she is a good filly back in form at the right time, winning the South East Oaks at this circuit and distance last start. BETTOR BEACH (13) was brilliant first up but worked and was hampered by a flat tyre last start. She is much better than that so include along with SEASIDE BOULEVARD (7) who came from a similar barrier to this when she won the Redcliffe Oaks last month.
